page_content:
BUAC701 Accounting Theory (3 Credits) Examines GAAP, its weaknesses and strengths, and the role that the FASB and SEC play in its development. Includes the FASB standard setting process and issues that relate to this process: FASB conceptual framework; lobbying activity; and impending FASB standards. Other topics include: how management incentives and ﬁrm type influence accounting choice; recognize versus disclose as a strategy; and current debates in accounting. While the user of accounting information is examined, this course focuses more on management and how it reports and discloses accounting information. Prerequisite: BMGT310, BMGT424, and BMGT311. Credit Only Granted for: BMGT706 or BUAC701. Formerly: BMGT706.

BUAC - Accounting and Information Assurance
